crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET MALWARE Rhadamanthys Stealer - Payload Download Request"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"GET"; http.header_names; content:"|0d 0a|X-CSRF-TOKEN|0d 0a|Cookie|0d 0a 0d 0a|"; endswith; http.cookie; content:"CSRF-TOKEN="; startswith; content:"==|3b 20|LANG=en-US"; distance:86; within:15; endswith; http.header.raw; pcre:"/X-CSRF-TOKEN\x3a\x20(?P<string>[a-zA-Z0-9\+\/]{86}\x3d\x3d)[\r\n]+Cookie\x3a\x20CSRF-TOKEN\x3d(?P=string)\x3b\x20LANG\x3den-US[\r\n]+$/D"; reference:url,threatmon.io/rhadamanthys-stealer-analysis-threatmon/; reference:md5,6c7f6d2ac093abd0edcd16e29de79dd5; classtype:command-and-control; sid:2043202; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2023_01_04, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, malware_family Rhadamanthys, performance_impact Low, signature_severity Major, updated_at 2023_01_04;)

trid.file_type: Excel Microsoft Office Open XML Format document

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)

attributes.magic: XML 1.0 document, Unicode text, UTF-8 text, with very long lines (2801u), with CRLF line terminators
ASCII text, with CRLF line terminators

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This code appears to be a list of IP addresses. There are 100 IP addresses in total. The IP addresses are all in the IPv4 format. There is no logic or flow control in the code. The code is likely used for malicious purposes, such as a botnet or a denial-of-service attack.
